messages.content:
hi @Alexander Indrajaya L team,
is there a way to inject custom secrets into the airflow dag? e.g. a Lark webhook url? since such info can't be committed into the repo.

E.g. can we update the ci/cd build script in mlops-cicd  to inject these as environment variables via gitlab ci/cd variables? Or any other better way of doing this?

cc @Dong Yajie

thread_replies.content:
currently we only have encrypted tfvars to store the secret 
CMIIW @Chen Zhike @Rezha Julio Arly Pradana 

can we update the ci/cd build script in mlops-cicd  to inject these as environment variables via gitlab ci/cd variables? Or any other better way of doing this?
or maybe we can have Ali KMS and mount it as a kubesecret and inject into airflow dag

or can add it via variables/connections in Airflow UI (only by people w/ permissions)

IMHO, I assume that secret is rarely updated, hence, you can ask MLOps team whenever you want add more secrets/variables

or maybe we can have Ali KMS and mount it as a kubesecret and inject into airflow dag
I assume if we do this, this secret will be injected into all airflow pipelines?
Cuz certain secrets are project specific, I was wondering if there's a way to scope it down.

It also makes it a lot harder to manage/deprecate these secrets long term, since it'll be hard to keep track of which pipelines are actively using what secret if the secrets are managed on an instance level rather than at dag level

you can ask MLOps team whenever you want add more secrets/variables
sure, this works with us. Primarily the issue above ^

I assume if we do this, this secret will be injected into all airflow pipelines?
Unless we can have some config in the job_config.yaml, to locate a specific Ali KMS Secret to mount into the DAG, need to exercise on the feasibility tho

Is this mandatory and urgent?

At least for now, the impetus is finding a way to send messages to Lark webhook url from within Airflow itself.
--
ps: actually as I was reviewing the mlops-cicd repo, I remembered that we also have the DOTENV file functionality which can be used to inject secrets into the environment, let me explore that solution first~
Yeah, I think you can use MGR_PROD_DOT_ENV_FILE (or any other DS project and env) in ci/cd variable and it wili be mounted as .env
